Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significant dyslipidemia (elevated total cholesterol, LDL, triglycerides; low HDL), hyperglycemia with normal HbA1c, and mildly elevated liver enzymes. Creatinine is elevated in one sample, suggesting possible renal impairment, but normal in another. Low total CO₂ may indicate mild metabolic acidosis. No significant proteinuria is present. Recommend further evaluation for metabolic syndrome, cardiovascular risk, and assessment of renal and liver function.
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
